summary: Iter-29 backend verification of (A) Admin Order Management lifecycle + assignment, (B) Shipper 'Accept Invoice' Net-14 invoicing, (C) Admin Invoice Management, plus regression for /api/orders/available. 24/25 pytest cases pass. Found and FIXED one CRITICAL backend bug (invoice creation returned 500 due to Motor injecting ObjectId `_id` into the returned dict — popped it before responding). Found one remaining backend bug: GET /api/invoices/{id}/pdf returns 500 with FPDFException 'Not enough horizontal space to render a single character' when the invoice has a long address — needs main-agent fix in _build_invoice_pdf (server.py ~5000). All invoice schema/idempotency/403/listing/PDF-content-type-not-applicable-yet, admin mark-paid/overdue/resend, admin invoicing settings GET+POST round-trip @ 12.5 then revert to 9.0, admin order lifecycle (assign→accepted, unassign→pending, pause, restore, complete→delivered+completed_at, fail w/ reason, notes append, assignment-history) verified. /api/orders/available regression returns 200 with >0 pending jobs.

critical.issue: 500 Internal Server Error -> fpdf.errors.FPDFException: 'Not enough horizontal space to render a single character'. Raised in /app/backend/server.py:5002 inside _build_invoice_pdf at pdf.multi_cell(0, 5, f'Delivery: ...'). Triggered by normal Helsinki dropoff_address used by the shipper-created order. Likely cause: a prior call to pdf.cell(0, 7, 'Order', new_x='LMARGIN', new_y='NEXT') / pdf.cell(120, ...) etc. leaves the current page/font/margin state such that the available width for multi_cell(w=0) is computed as <= 0 for that line. Suggested fix: explicitly pass a non-zero width (e.g. pdf.epw or 0 after calling pdf.set_x(pdf.l_margin)), or set pdf.set_left_margin/right_margin defaults before multi_cell, or wrap long lines with pdf.cell(0, 5, ..., new_x='LMARGIN', new_y='NEXT'). PDF endpoint is currently unusable for any invoice whose dropoff_address triggers the wrap.

minor.issue: FIXED in this run. Was 500 because Motor's insert_one mutates the inserted dict in place to add `_id: ObjectId(...)`, then FastAPI failed jsonable_encoder. Fix applied at /app/backend/server.py ~line 4948-4952: doc.pop('_id', None) after insert_one (before responding/updating). All four downstream surfaces that return that same `inv` doc (GET /api/invoices/{id}, GET /api/shipper/invoices, GET /api/admin/invoices, POST mark-paid/overdue) read from a fresh find_one({}, {'_id': 0}) so they were not impacted.

Returns the shipper's id under the key `driver_id` (LoginResponse model is reused across driver/shipper/admin). Cosmetic, but confusing for new SDKs — consider adding an explicit `shipper_id` alias to the response. Reference: /app/backend/server.py shipper_login.

action_items: (HIGH) Fix invoice PDF generation in /app/backend/server.py:_build_invoice_pdf (~line 4998-5002). Replace pdf.multi_cell(0, 5, ...) with an explicit width (e.g. pdf.epw or 180) or call pdf.set_x(pdf.l_margin) immediately before each multi_cell so the available width is positive. Re-test by hitting GET /api/invoices/{id}/pdf for any newly created invoice — should return 200 application/pdf, body starts with %PDF.
(Optional) Add `shipper_id` alias to /api/auth/shipper-login response to disambiguate from driver_id.
critical_code_review_comments: /app/backend/server.py:4948-4952 (_create_invoice_for_order) — Motor mutates the inserted dict to inject ObjectId `_id`. Without the pop, FastAPI serialization fails for the FIRST call only (idempotent replays read fresh from DB with projection {_id:0} so they succeed). All other invoice-returning endpoints already project _id out — good.

/app/backend/server.py:4898-4906 (_next_invoice_number) — Uses settings.find_one_and_update with $inc/upsert/return_document=True; correctly produces sequential NDR-YYYY-NNNN numbers (verified: NDR-2026-1002..1005 observed).

/app/backend/server.py:5057-5077 (get_invoice_pdf) — Dual-auth (header OR ?token=) is reasonable for direct browser downloads, but make sure the OpenAPI schema clearly marks token as optional. Currently HTTPBearer is still required even when ?token= is supplied; only works because credentials is optional via the dependency default. Verified.

/app/backend/server.py:4723-4814 (admin order lifecycle endpoints) — assign/unassign/pause/restore/complete/fail all behave as advertised. Note: /complete and /fail do NOT validate the current status (e.g. you can /fail a delivered order). The Iter-29 spec asked for this looser behavior, so this is fine — flagging for awareness.

Note: /shipper/shipments returns `order_id` (not `id`) in its create response, while admin/lifecycle endpoints return objects with `id`. Test harness normalises this; consider standardising the field name.
updated_files: /app/backend/server.py (added doc.pop('_id', None) after db.invoices.insert_one in _create_invoice_for_order, line ~4950-4953)
/app/backend/tests/test_iter29_invoicing_and_order_mgmt.py (new — 25 pytest cases covering all 7 features listed in the review request)

success_rate.backend: 96% (24/25 pytest cases — only PDF endpoint failing)

seed_data_creation: POST /api/seed-demo invoked (idempotent). Created ~6 throwaway demo shipper orders via /api/shipper/shipments (Mannerheimintie 1 -> Hämeentie 30, ~4.4km, ~EUR 18.20). One throwaway TEST_OtherCo shipper registered per pytest fixture run for 403 tests. One invoice marked paid, one order set to delivered, one to failed, one to paused/restored. Demo data not disturbed.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Re-run pytest /app/backend/tests/test_iter29_invoicing_and_order_mgmt.py after main agent fixes _build_invoice_pdf — only test_invoice_pdf is currently failing. Set EXPO_PUBLIC_BACKEND_URL env var (script reads it). Test fixture is module-scoped; do NOT change scope or the lifecycle order chain (assign->unassign->pause->restore->complete) will break. Shipper login returns shipper id under 'driver_id' key — gotcha. /api/shipper/shipments POST returns 'order_id' not 'id'; helper _create_shipment normalises this. Two real Google Directions API calls per test run; uses caching after first.
rca_of_issue: Bug 1 (FIXED): POST /api/shipper/shipments/{id}/accept-invoice returned 500. Root cause: Motor (motor.motor_asyncio.AsyncIOMotorCollection.insert_one) mutates the inserted dict in place by adding a bson.ObjectId under the key '_id'. The endpoint then returned that same dict; FastAPI's jsonable_encoder cannot serialise ObjectId and raises TypeError('ObjectId object is not iterable'). Idempotent replays returned the doc fetched via find_one(..., {'_id': 0}) so they would have worked — but the FIRST call always 500'd. Fix: `doc.pop('_id', None)` right after `db.invoices.insert_one(doc)` in _create_invoice_for_order. Bug 2 (OPEN): /api/invoices/{id}/pdf raises FPDFException 'Not enough horizontal space to render a single character' at server.py:5002 inside _build_invoice_pdf. pdf.multi_cell(0, ...) computes available width = page_width - left_margin - right_margin - current_x; the preceding `pdf.cell(0, 7, 'Order', new_x='LMARGIN', new_y='NEXT')` should reset x to LMARGIN, but the combination of font metrics + the previous narrow-cell row leaves an effective width <= 0 for some addresses (reproduced 100% on the demo Helsinki shipper-order). Recommended fix: replace multi_cell(0, …) with multi_cell(pdf.epw, …) or call pdf.set_x(pdf.l_margin) before each multi_cell, or pass an explicit width like 180.
